sentiment.rationale: Jonah flees from the Lord's command; a great storm threatens the ship; the sailors cast Jonah overboard after his confession.
Jonah's prayer from the belly of the fish is a deeply penitential psalm of distress, remembrance of the Lord, and thanksgiving for deliverance.
Jonah preaches destruction to Nineveh; the entire city repents in sackcloth and ashes, and God relents from the threatened judgment.
Jonah is angry that God spared Nineveh; the Lord teaches him compassion through the gourd, worm, and east wind.

missingCharacters.name: King of Nineveh
missingCharacters.description: The unnamed king of Nineveh who led his people in repentance when Jonah prophesied the city's destruction.

summaries.summary: The Lord calls Jonah to cry against Nineveh; Jonah flees by ship to Tarshish, a great storm arises, and the sailors reluctantly cast him into the sea.
From inside the great fish, Jonah offers a psalm of desperate prayer and thanksgiving, vowing to pay what he has promised; the fish vomits him onto dry land.
Jonah preaches to Nineveh that it will be overthrown in forty days; the entire city from king to cattle repents in sackcloth, and God spares them.
Jonah is displeased that Nineveh is spared; God uses a gourd, a worm, and a scorching wind to teach Jonah about divine compassion for all people.

crossReferences.note: Jesus cites Jonah's three days and nights in the whale as a sign of His own burial and resurrection.
Jonah's cry from the depths echoes the psalmist calling upon the Lord in distress.
Jesus declares Nineveh repented at the preaching of Jonah, and a greater than Jonah is present.
Jonah quotes God's self-declaration as gracious, merciful, slow to anger, and of great kindness.

verses.reason: The Lord prepares a great fish to swallow Jonah — a type and shadow of Christ's three days in the tomb.
'Out of the belly of hell cried I, and thou heardest my voice' — Jonah's prayer from the depths of despair.
'Salvation is of the Lord' — the central theological statement of the book.
Nineveh's wholesale repentance — one of the greatest mass conversions in scripture.
God's rhetorical question about caring for 120,000 people who cannot discern right from left — a powerful statement of divine compassion.
